Description: Automatically resolve Eloquent models from route parameters by type-hinting in controller methods
Do: Use implicit route model binding with type hints. Customize route key with getRouteKeyName()
Dont: Manually fetch models by ID in every controller method

Description: Use apiResource() for RESTful routes which excludes create/edit form routes
Do: Use Route::apiResource() for API controllers. Group related routes with prefix and middleware
Dont: Define each route manually for standard CRUD operations

Description: Always eagerly load relationships to prevent N+1 queries
Do: Use with() for known relationships. Use load() for conditional loading. Use withCount() for counts
Dont: Load relationships lazily inside loops

Description: Encapsulate reusable query constraints in local scopes on the model
Do: Define reusable query logic as scopeXxx methods on models
Dont: Repeat the same where clauses across controllers and services
Code Bad: // Repeated everywhere:\nPost::where('status', 'published')->whereNotNull('published_at')->get();\n// Same conditions duplicated in 10 places

Description: Use Form Request classes to handle validation and authorization per endpoint
Do: Create dedicated FormRequest classes for each write endpoint. Use authorize() for permissions
Dont: Validate in controllers with manual if/else checks

Description: Create middleware for cross-cutting concerns like logging rate limiting and request transformation
Do: Keep middleware focused on one concern. Register in route groups not globally
Dont: Put business logic in middleware

Description: Use JSON Resources to transform models into consistent API responses with controlled field exposure
Do: Create Resource classes per model for API responses. Use ResourceCollection for lists
Dont: Return raw models or manual arrays from controllers
Code Bad: return response()->json($post);\n// Exposes all columns: password hashes, internal IDs, etc.

Description: Decouple side effects from main business logic using events and listeners
Do: Use events for notifications cache invalidation logging analytics
Dont: Call side effects directly in controllers or services creating tight coupling

Description: Dispatch time-consuming tasks to background queues for faster responses
Do: Queue emails PDF generation file processing and external API calls
Dont: Process heavy tasks synchronously in the request cycle

Description: Use Laravel Sanctum for API token and SPA authentication
Do: Use Sanctum for SPA (cookie-based) and mobile/API (token-based) auth
Dont: Build custom JWT auth when Sanctum covers your needs

Description: Use policies for model-based authorization and gates for general permissions
Do: Create Policy per model. Use $this->authorize() in controllers. Register policies in AuthServiceProvider
Dont: Check permissions with if/else in every controller method

Description: Write reversible migrations with proper column types and indexes
Do: Make all migrations reversible. Add indexes on foreign keys and filtered columns. Use descriptive names
Dont: Write irreversible migrations or modify existing migrations after deployment

Description: Use factories for test data generation and seeders for default application data
Do: Define factories with realistic Faker data. Use states for variations. Separate seeders by concern
Dont: Create test data manually or use raw SQL inserts

Description: Use the Notification system for sending mail SMS Slack and database notifications
Do: Use Notification classes with via() method. Queue notifications. Store in database for in-app
Dont: Send notifications by directly calling Mail or SMS services
Code Bad: Mail::to($user)->send(new OrderShippedMail($order));\n// Only email, no database record, not queued

Description: Register services and bindings in dedicated service providers
Do: Create focused service providers per feature. Use register() for bindings boot() for setup
Dont: Put all bindings in AppServiceProvider making it a god class

Description: Write comprehensive feature tests for API endpoints
Do: Test happy path error cases auth and response structure. Use actingAs() RefreshDatabase
Dont: Only test happy paths or only unit test without integration

Description: Cache entire HTTP responses for read-heavy endpoints
Do: Use spatie/laravel-responsecache or manual Cache in middleware for expensive list endpoints
Dont: Compute every response from scratch on every request

Description: Use single-purpose Action classes for complex business operations
Do: One action class per business operation. Invokable with __invoke or execute method
Dont: Bloated service classes with dozens of methods

Description: Configure the Laravel exception handler for consistent API error responses
Do: Map domain exceptions to proper HTTP status codes and JSON format in the Handler
Dont: Let exceptions render as HTML or with inconsistent formats
Code Bad: // Default handler: HTML whoops page for API requests\n// Or no custom rendering:\n// GET /api/posts/999 returns HTML 404 page to API client

Description: Run optimization commands during deployment for maximum performance
Do: Cache config routes views and events. Install without dev deps. Optimize autoloader
Dont: Deploy without any caching or optimization
Code Good:
# deploy.sh:
composer install --no-dev --optimize-autoloader
php artisan config:cache
php artisan route:cache
php artisan view:cache
php artisan event:cache
php artisan migrate --force
php artisan queue:restart
Code Bad:
# No optimization:
composer install
php artisan migrate
# Config parsed from .env on every request
# Routes compiled on every request
# Views compiled on first use

Description: Use Laravel's scheduler for cron-like periodic tasks managed in code
Do: Define schedules in console.php or Console Kernel. Use withoutOverlapping() for safety
Dont: Use raw cron entries for Laravel tasks
Code Bad:
# Multiple cron entries:
# 0 * * * * php artisan orders:process-expired
# 0 6 * * * php artisan reports:generate-daily
# Cron entries scattered, hard to manage

Description: Use cache tags to group related cache entries for surgical invalidation
Do: Tag cache entries by model or domain. Flush specific tags on data changes
Dont: Flush all cache or use individual key deletion

Description: Use Blade components for reusable UI elements with typed props
Do: Create class-based components for complex logic. Use anonymous components for simple HTML
Dont: Copy-paste HTML snippets across views

Description: Use Eloquent casts and accessors for automatic type transformation
Do: Use $casts for type conversion. Use Attribute accessors for computed properties
Dont: Transform data manually in every controller or view

Description: Organize configuration files properly with environment-aware defaults
Do: Use config files for structured settings. Use env() only in config files. Use config() everywhere else
Dont: Call env() directly in application code

Description: Version your API routes and controllers for backward compatibility
Do: Prefix routes with version. Create versioned controller directories
Dont: Break existing API clients by changing response format without versioning
Code Bad: Route::apiResource('posts', PostController::class);\n// Breaking change affects all clients immediately\n// /api/posts response format changed without warning

Description: Use SoftDeletes trait for recoverable deletion with proper query scoping
Do: Add SoftDeletes trait. Force delete when truly permanent. Filter in global scope
Dont: Use hard deletes for data that might need recovery

Description: Enable preventLazyLoading in development to catch N+1 queries early
Do: Call Model::preventLazyLoading() in AppServiceProvider boot for non-production
Dont: Leave lazy loading unchecked in development discovering N+1 in production

Description: Use Laravel Pipeline for sequential multi-step processing
Do: Chain processing steps for request transformations or data processing workflows
Dont: Nest processing steps with deep if/else chains

Description: Use Laravel Collection methods for expressive data manipulation
Do: Use map filter reduce groupBy sortBy etc instead of manual loops
Dont: Write foreach loops with temporary arrays for data transformation
Code Good:
$summary = $orders
    ->groupBy('status')
    ->map(fn($group) => [
        'count' => $group->count(),
        'total' => $group->sum('amount'),
        'avg' => round($group->avg('amount'), 2),
    ])
    ->sortByDesc('total');
